DISCOVERY 302, NON-PARTY/THIRD-PARTY CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND DEFENDANT ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS RE-NOTICE OF MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA'S DEPOSITION SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THE CCSF SHALL PROVIDE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER, WHICH DO NOT REFLECT THE THOUGHTS AND IMPRESSIONS OF THE INDIVIDUALS WHO INVESTIGATED ANY SUCH COMPLAINTS. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER RE: AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. BOTH SIDES ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. DISCOVERY 302, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RE DEPOSITION ON S.F. ADULT PROBATION IS GRANTED. JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL IS INAPPLICABLE DUE TO THE DISSIMILAR SITUATIONS OF THE PARTIES. THE INFORMATION SOUGHT FROM THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT GOES WELL BEYOND WHAT DEFENDANT MAY NEED TO IMPEACH PLAINTIFF. SANCTIONS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S POSITION, WHILE REJECTED, DID NOT LACK SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND SUPPLEMENTATION OF PRIVILEGE LOG. Appearances: Marc R. Lewis of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3921) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted in part as to special interrogatory 1. Plaintiff shall provide further responses but need not include facilities where plaintiff received mental health care. Granted as to special interrogatories 9, 15, and 19. These requests seek relevant information. Granted in part as to form interrogatory 12.1 and special interrogatory 12. Plaintiff need not identify every individual who may have knowledge of the incident, but shall provide a list of witnesses to the incident known to him. Denied as to special interrogatories 23 and 24. These requests are no longer relevant. Denied as to special interrogatories 33 and 34. Plaintiff provided all of the responsive information that he has. Denied as to form interrogatory 12.2. A list of witness interviewed by counsel is privileged because it "would necessarily reflect counsel's evaluation of the case by revealing which witnesses or persons who claimed knowledge of the incident counsel deemed important enough to interview." (Nacht & Lewis Architects, Inc. v. Superior Court, (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 214, 217). Granted as to the privilege log. The purpose of a privilege log is to provide a factual description of documents substantiating a claim of privilege and to aid the Court in identifying whether there is a privilege. A privilege log that fails to identify specific documents is meaningless. Requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

Notice O Fmotion And Otion For Leave Of Court To Obtain Mental Examination Under 2032.310
Off Calendar Nov-14-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendant Elias Georgopoulos' motion to compel the the mental examination of plaintiff Rogelio Hechavarria pursuant to CCP Section 2032.310.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Debbie Acevedo, #7692.

Joseph S. May, Esq. & Arnold I. Berschler, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Rogelio Hechavarria, who was not present at today's hearing.
Blake Loebs, Esq. appeared for defendant Elias Georgopoulos, who was not present at today's hearing.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

(1) Plaintiff's second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is dismissed with prejudice;
(2) Plaintiff may not seek compensation for an mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed and
(3) No expert testimony regarding this usual mental and emotional distress will be presented at trial in support of the claim for damages.

Proposed order was submitted to and signed by the Court.
